2022-06-01.title: ‘We Are About to Lose a Lot More Lives and Limbs’: Ukraine Girds for a Wave of Amputees
2022-06-01.abstract: The war in Ukraine is pushing the country to expand its prosthetics industry amid fears of looming shortages.

2022-06-01.abstract: Amid the roar of artillery and bone-rattling explosions, New York Times photographers have borne graphic witness to the fight to survive. These are their stories and images.

2022-06-01.abstract: With daily infections in low double digits, there’s pressure to shake the city back to life, reviving the factories, companies and supply lines vital to China’s economy.

2022-06-01.title: Germany promises to send Ukraine a missile defense system and radar equipment.
2022-06-01.abstract: The announcement came as Chancellor Olaf Scholz faced pressure over when the government would deliver on previous weapons pledges.

2022-06-01.title: Can Ancient Maori Knowledge Aid Science? Ask These Freshwater Crayfish.

2022-06-02.abstract: Britain is honoring the monarch’s 70 years on the throne with four days of events.

2022-06-02.abstract: As the United States moved to ship ever more advanced weapon systems to Ukraine to aid it in its fight to thwart Russia’s eastern offensive, Moscow said the latest move by President Biden threatened to escalate the war in dangerous ways.

2022-06-02.abstract: LONDON — With regimented lines of Scots and Irish guards, throngs of Union Jack-clad onlookers and waves of aircraft streaking overhead, Queen Elizabeth II celebrated 70 years on the throne on Thursday, earning tributes from world leaders and ordinary people for a reign unmatched in British history.

2022-06-02.title: Queen Elizabeth’s Platinum Jubilee: Seven Decades in Photos

2022-06-02.abstract: Celebrations of Queen Elizabeth II’s milestones have been national moments of reflection and celebration.

2022-06-03.title: As Targeted Killings Spike, Hindus Are Desperate to Flee Kashmir
2022-06-03.abstract: Decades after militant violence forced many of them out of the disputed region, hundreds of families have left in another exodus.

2022-06-03.title: Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.
2022-06-03.abstract: As the war in Ukraine marked its 100th day, Africa was urgently seeking relief from the disruptions of critical supplies of grain and other staple foods. Some of the world’s poorest countries face alarming levels of hunger and starvation and the United Nations has warned that Russia’s naval blockade of Ukraine could lead to famines around the world.

2022-06-03.title: From the Bones of Victims, a Doctor Unearths a Drug War’s True Toll
2022-06-03.abstract: Raquel Fortun, one of only two forensic pathologists in the Philippines, is using her skills to show how other doctors falsely claimed some victims of the country’s drug war had died natural deaths.

2022-06-03.title: What is Great Paul? The story behind the iconic bell at St. Paul’s Cathedral.
2022-06-03.abstract: The London cathedral’s bronze bell, weighing over 16 tons, is the largest bell ever cast in the British Isles.

2022-06-04.abstract: Throngs of admirers cheered Queen Elizabeth II at her Platinum Jubilee appearance on Thursday, but she was unable to attend a service on Friday. Prince Charles, in a familiar role, played the understudy.

2022-06-04.title: Tanned, Buff and Ready to Pose, Gaza Bodybuilders Feel Weight of Blockade
2022-06-04.abstract: Bodybuilding is popular among Palestinians living in Gaza, but travel restrictions and other obstacles as a result of the Israeli blockade make competing abroad a struggle.

2022-06-04.abstract: Most farmers have fled the Russian advance, but Oleksandr Chaplik has stayed, despite the lethal risks and a lack of workers, water and power.

2022-06-04.abstract: The handgun market in Canada has been frozen as Parliament reviews sweeping legal changes, including a mandatory buyback of some rifles.

2022-06-04.abstract: The evacuation of the square in Central London rattled the capital on the third day of Queen Elizabeth’s Platinum Jubilee. The police later said it was not related to terrorism.

2022-06-05.title: Ukraine aims to restart soccer leagues on its own soil in August.
2022-06-05.abstract: In an interview with The Associated Press, the leader of Ukraine’s soccer federation said President Volodymyr Zelensky had approved the decision.

2022-06-05.abstract: The North’s latest test, involving eight missiles, came a day after the United States and South Korea finished joint naval exercises in the East China Sea.

2022-06-05.abstract: A few years ago, the nation vowed to rid itself of most imported predators. But now some people are asking if that goal is feasible, or worth what it will cost.

2022-06-05.abstract: Backed by the Kremlin, the shadowy network known as the Wagner Group is getting rich in Sudan while helping the military to crush a democracy movement.

2022-06-05.abstract: A fire set off a series of explosions, killing at least 49 people, including firefighters. Many others were in critical condition.

2022-06-06.title: Assassinations Become Weapon of Choice for Guerrilla Groups in Myanmar
2022-06-06.abstract: A movement to restore democracy has evolved into deadly warfare between a ruthless military and a resistance force with limited weaponry.

2022-06-06.abstract: The storm brought rain and strong winds to Bermuda before becoming a post-tropical cyclone.

2022-06-07.title: Britain’s prime minister faces potential pitfalls even after victory in a no-confidence vote over lockdown parties.

Boris Johnson’s less-than-convincing victory in a no-confidence vote by his own party on Monday leaves him badly damaged, with few obvious ways to resurrect his fortunes and plenty of opportunities for coup plotters.

2022-06-08.abstract: Eight years after the Sewol ferry disaster took the lives of 250 South Korean students, parents say they are still struggling to come to terms with the lessons the tragedy brought to bear.

2022-06-08.abstract: As Russian forces bear down on eastern Ukraine, a small army of volunteers is racing to bring to safety those unable to care for themselves.

2022-06-08.abstract: The new Labor government has established a ministerial position to begin the process of making the country a republic. But the idea is far from the top of its agenda.

2022-06-09.abstract: Australia and Canada said Chinese jets harassed their military planes, though Beijing says it was defending its security. A mishap over Pacific waters could have ominous repercussions.

2022-06-09.abstract: The health minister and the mayor of Hanoi were removed from their posts in a scandal that has led to charges against dozens of officials.

2022-06-09.abstract: A nation of beer lovers is facing a shortage of bottles, partly because of the war in Ukraine. Breweries are looking to drinkers for a rescue.

2022-06-09.abstract: An American woman and her companion caused $27,000 in damage to the landmark, officials said. They were fined and have been barred from the site for six months.

2022-06-10.title: When Cockatoos Become Cocka-Too Much
2022-06-10.abstract: The native Australian birds are charismatic and deeply destructive.

2022-06-10.abstract: More than 300 Ukrainians, 77 of them children, were imprisoned in a dank and airless school basement for nearly a month. Ten of them died.

2022-06-10.abstract: The postponement of the trip to the Democratic Republic of Congo and South Sudan comes as speculation swirls that the pontiff may be thinking of stepping down.

2022-06-10.abstract: The Gloucester, which was carrying a future king of England when it ran aground, was found 15 years ago. Its discovery was revealed this week.

2022-06-11.abstract: The trial of Jeanine Añez, who took power after Evo Morales was ousted, has led to concerns about politicians’ use of the justice system to target opponents.

2022-06-11.title: Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.

Putin.

2022-06-11.title: Recording India’s Linguistic Riches as Leaders Push Hindi as Nation’s Tongue
2022-06-11.abstract: Ganesh Devy has spent decades documenting India’s hundreds of distinct languages. Up next: a 12,000-year history of India he hopes will help counter the government’s Hindu-first campaign.

2022-06-11.abstract: The worst drought in four decades and a sharp rise in food prices caused by the war in Ukraine have left almost half of Somalia’s people facing acute food shortages.

2022-06-12.abstract: The restoration of a wall painting depicting an idealized Jerusalem is a reminder that the Roman monument, known best for gladiatorial combat, was a sacred Christian space for centuries.

2022-06-13.abstract: Why does war’s wreckage — the downed helicopters, the destroyed tanks and the dead — draw crowds? A former Marine ponders the question as he stands before the badly burned body of a Russian fighter.

2022-06-13.abstract: The announcement by Michelle Bachelet, the U.N. high commissioner for human rights, came a month after she was criticized by rights groups for traveling to China.

2022-06-13.abstract: The powerful Shiite cleric Muqtada al-Sadr, who controls the largest bloc in Parliament, has directed dozens of lawmakers loyal to him to resign.

Official Inquiries About Grenfell Tower Blaze Go On, 5 Years After the Fire
2022-06-14.abstract: The fire quickly engulfed the apartment building, fueled by cladding now banned. An investigation is continuing. But the families of the 72 who died find accountability slow in coming.

2022-06-14.abstract: Hundreds of millions of humanity’s most vulnerable live in South Asia, where rising temperatures make it more difficult to address poverty, food insecurity and health challenges.

2022-06-15.abstract: The police arrested a second suspect after Dom Phillips, a British journalist, and Bruno Araújo Pereira, a Brazilian expert on Indigenous people, went missing.

2022-06-15.abstract: Since the start of Russia’s invasion, the Institute for the Study of War has provided daily updates about the situation on the front. The assessments from the Washington-based research group have become required reading for people following the war.

2022-06-16.abstract: The two sexual assault charges, against an officer who had been slated to lead the army, are the latest in a cascade of cases and investigations.

2022-06-16.abstract: LVIV, Ukraine — Older men and women and young mothers clutching their children’s hands gingerly made their way down the steep steps of a blue train car on Wednesday afternoon.

2022-06-17.abstract: Over many years and locations, we had come to almost worship the World Cup, something that let us bond with almost anyone, anywhere.

2022-06-17.abstract: The band’s label saw its stock price plunge, and the possibility that the K-pop group won’t tour as pandemic restrictions ease threatens to reverberate through South Korea’s economy.

2022-06-17.abstract: The Commission’s blessing is just the first step on a long road. The final decision will be in the hands of European Union leaders, who will meet June 23 and 24 in Brussels to tackle the thorny question.

2022-06-18.abstract: The people toiling in Ukraine’s cemeteries and morgues bear a silent burden as they handle the war’s growing toll.

2022-06-18.abstract: The police chief in an Indian island territory had a theory: If the boss was watching his officers’ weight, they would too.

2022-06-18.title: Russia: Deaths veiled in secrecy.
2022-06-18.abstract: In Russia, news of death arrives stealthily.

2022-06-18.abstract: When political leaders face a constitutional crisis, like that of Jan. 6, the process of collectively deciding how to respond can be messy, arbitrary, and sometimes change the nature of the system itself.

2022-06-19.abstract: A New York Times analysis of visual evidence from Ukraine showed widespread use by Russia of cluster weapons banned under certain international treaties.

2022-06-19.abstract: LVIV, Ukraine — President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine on Saturday morning made an unannounced visit to Mykolaiv, a southern Ukrainian city battered by the war that has been held up by Kyiv as a sign of fierce resistance, and the nearby port city of Odesa.

2022-06-19.title: The Odesa opera house reopens, defying Russia’s aggression.
2022-06-19.abstract: ODESA, Ukraine — In a nation at war, and a city aching for some semblance of normality, the Odesa Opera reopened for the first time since the Russian invasion began, asserting civilization against the barbarism unleashed from Moscow.

2022-06-19.abstract: It has been two months since the Kremlin began its large-scale assault on the eastern Donbas region of Ukraine, directing the force of its vast arsenal along a 300-mile front and inflicting heavy losses on Ukrainian forces as its fighters blasted their way to grueling gains.

2022-06-19.title: Western leaders warn that the war in Ukraine could last years.
2022-06-19.abstract: Jens Stoltenberg said that Ukrainian forces were fighting bravely, and that with the arrival of more modern weaponry, they would be able to push Russian troops out of the eastern Donbas region.

2022-06-20.abstract: The Ukrainian Parliament voted to ban some Russian media and sent two bills to President Zelensky to sign.

2022-06-20.abstract: The former rebel and longtime senator’s victory sets the third largest nation in Latin America on a sharply new path.

2022-06-20.abstract: Led by young women from Eastern Europe, they are cornering Europe’s leaders and pressing them for a total energy embargo on Russia — to end the fighting and to save the planet.

2022-06-20.abstract: Thousands of refugees from Ukraine have been sent to so-called filtration camps, where they have been interrogated and then forced to resettle to Russia. Some Ukrainians escaped to Estonia. They told us their stories.

2022-06-20.abstract: BRUSSELS — Emmanuel Macron, the president of France, had just finished a speech at a major conference on Europe.

2022-06-21.abstract: During his campaign, Gustavo Petro proposed major reforms if he was elected. After winning Sunday’s presidential election, he’ll now have to prove he can carry out those changes.

2022-06-21.abstract: Several villages near the town of Bankass were attacked over the weekend by jihadists, officials said.

2022-06-21.abstract: When Russia shifted its military campaign to focus on eastern Ukraine this spring, senior officials in the Biden administration said the next four to six weeks of fighting would determine the war’s eventual path.

2022-06-21.abstract: Travel was disrupted for tens of millions of people during the country’s biggest walkout in decades as union leaders warned of a summer of labor unrest.

2022-06-22.abstract: Without its Black Sea coast, a landlocked Ukraine would struggle. Mykolaiv is determined that won’t happen.

2022-06-22.abstract: The Ukrainian military said it was attacking Russian infrastructure on Snake Island.

2022-06-22.abstract: Namibian investigators tracing money transfers by men suspected of stealing wads of cash from President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa say their cross-border calls for help went unanswered.

2022-06-23.abstract: WASHINGTON — A new examination of how Russia used its cybercapabilities in the first months of the war in Ukraine contains a number of surprises: Moscow conducted more cyberattacks than was realized at the time to bolster its invasion, but more than two-thirds of them failed, echoing its poor performance on the physical battlefield.

2022-06-23.title: From Russian Elites, No Sign of Broad Challenge to Putin
2022-06-23.abstract: Many business owners and intellectuals fled Russia after the invasion of Ukraine, but others stayed behind, struggling to redefine their lives. Their diverging choices do not add up to a broad challenge to Vladimir Putin’s war.

2022-06-23.title: As Russian forces fortify a Ukrainian nuclear power plant, employees are fleeing repression, local officials say.
2022-06-23.abstract: The Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant, Europe’s largest, lies in southern Ukraine in a strategically important spot.

2022-06-23.title: Extreme Weather Hits China With Massive Floods and Scorching Heat
2022-06-23.abstract: Hundreds of thousands of people have been displaced by the historic flooding. Elsewhere, record-high temperatures are rupturing roads and driving energy consumption.

2022-06-23.title: European leaders are meeting today to decide on granting Ukraine E.U. candidate status.
2022-06-23.abstract: Their approval would allow Ukraine to be a candidate to join the bloc, starting what could be a long road.

2022-06-24.title: Young soccer players rushed out of Ukraine find themselves in a lonely limbo.
2022-06-24.abstract: SPLIT, Croatia — It was in their moment of triumph, when they had beaten their opponents and come together to collect their medals, when some of the boys were overcome with sadness, when the tears welled in their eyes.

2022-06-24.title: In Warsaw Park, Ukraine’s Teen Refugees Hang Out and Hang On
2022-06-24.abstract: Some fled to Poland’s capital alone. All feel deeply uneasy about the future. For the young Ukrainians packing into Poland’s capital, a park offers a place where they can try to cope, together.

2022-06-24.title: Conservatives Lose 2 Races in U.K., Adding to Pressure on Boris Johnson
2022-06-24.abstract: The double defeat exposed the party’s vulnerabilities and was likely to revive talk of another no-confidence vote against the prime minister.

2022-06-24.title: Ukraine’s foreign minister sees E.U. candidacy as a crucial step toward joining a ‘liberal empire.’
2022-06-24.abstract: “History has been made,” he said of Ukraine’s being accepted as a candidate for E.U. membership.

2022-06-24.title: After Years of Acrimony, China and Australia Cautiously Reach Out
2022-06-24.abstract: The arrival of a new government in Canberra has set the stage for ministerial talks and overtures for easing tensions. Plenty still divides the two nations.

2022-06-25.title: 2 Killed and at Least 19 Wounded in Shooting in Norway’s Capital
2022-06-25.abstract: The shooting happened near a popular gay club in downtown Oslo, on the weekend when the city is set to host its Pride parade.

2022-06-25.title: Occupied Mariupol is suffering deeply, its mayor says.
2022-06-25.abstract: Mariupol, a Ukrainian port city that became emblematic of the savagery of Russia’s military campaign, is now suffering deeply under Russian rule, according to the city’s mayor.

2022-06-25.title: Coal Dust and Methane Below, Russian Bombs Above
2022-06-25.abstract: The war has added a new threat to the dangerous lives of miners in eastern Ukraine.

2022-06-25.title: Belarus’s Leader, ‘Europe’s Last Dictator,’ Burnishes Links to Russia
2022-06-25.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia  met with President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko of Belarus on Saturday amid questions over how far Minsk will allow itself to be drawn into the war.

2022-06-25.title: Norway Mass Shooting Is Being Investigated as Terrorism, Police Say
2022-06-25.abstract: The attack, which killed two people and seriously wounded 10 others near a gay club in Oslo, came hours before the city’s Pride parade, which has now been canceled.

2022-06-26.title: Here is what foreign-policy experts are discussing ahead of the G7 meeting.
2022-06-26.abstract: Before Russia invaded Ukraine, this year’s Group of 7 summit was supposed to provide an opportunity to change gears from typical geopolitics and finance, and focus on the future. The top themes were meant to be climate, public health amid the coronavirus pandemic and equity around the globe.

2022-06-26.title: Biden’s new task is to ready allies for a long conflict in Ukraine.
2022-06-26.abstract: President Biden will be trying to unite his allies on new ways to isolate Moscow.

2022-06-26.title: Rising food and fuel prices loom over the summit.
2022-06-26.abstract: Hovering over the Group of 7 meeting and the NATO summit that follows it is a perilous moment for the global economy, probably the most tenuous since the 2008-09 financial crisis.

2022-06-26.title: The U.S. and Britain will ban imports of gold from Russia.
2022-06-26.abstract: President Biden announced the decision before the start of a G7 summit meeting in Germany.

2022-06-26.title: France’s Far Right Surges Into Parliament, and Further Into the Mainstream
2022-06-26.abstract: Marine Le Pen’s National Rally now has a place of power in the political establishment and a chance to prove itself in the eyes of voters.

2022-06-27.title: Colombian Bullfight Stand Collapse Kills at Least 6
2022-06-27.abstract: One child was reported dead when a spectator section collapsed at a bullfighting festival, and more than a hundred were reported injured.

2022-06-27.title: U.S. officials say the G7 is close to an agreement on price caps on Russian oil.
2022-06-27.abstract: KRUN, Germany — Leaders of the Group of 7 nations are close to an agreement in principle to begin the process of potentially imposing price caps on Russian oil and are set to impose other new economic penalties on Moscow to support Ukraine, Biden administration officials said on Monday.

2022-06-27.title: Europe and the U.S. court India to join efforts to punish Russia.
2022-06-27.abstract: Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India has maintained a neutral stance on the war.

2022-06-27.title: Civilians should immediately evacuate a Ukrainian city that is under fire, a regional official warned.
2022-06-27.abstract: Residents of Lysychansk, across the river from a city that fell to Russian control last week, should leave over “the real threat to life and health,” the region’s military governor said.

2022-06-27.title: Spectator Stand at Colombian Bullfight Collapses, Killing at Least 6
2022-06-27.abstract: At least six people were killed and more than a hundred were reported injured when a wooden spectator section collapsed at a bullfighting festival in Colombia.

2022-06-28.title: NATO says it will significantly increase troops assigned to its eastern flank.
2022-06-28.abstract: NATO is set to approve its biggest overhaul of its defenses since the Cold War, significantly increasing the number of troops assigned to defend the alliance’s eastern flank and making a major commitment to position heavy military equipment there.

2022-06-28.title: The NATO summit in Madrid could see fissures and debate beneath a semblance of unity.
2022-06-28.abstract: BRUSSELS — After the collapse of the Soviet Union, some asked whether NATO had any real reason to exist. But Russia’s invasion of Ukraine gave new urgency to NATO as a defensive alliance aimed at deterring a bellicose Moscow.

2022-06-28.title: A cap on the price of Russian oil comes with many questions.
2022-06-28.abstract: The idea is to limit how much Russia can earn from the oil it sells while still keeping markets well supplied.

2022-06-28.title: G7 Leaders Promise $4.5 Billion to Address Global Hunger Caused by War in Ukraine
2022-06-28.abstract: The planned commitment, more than half of it from the U.S., reflects worldwide alarm at shortages in wheat and sunflower oil caused by Russia’s invasion.

2022-06-28.title: On Front Lines, Communication Breakdowns Prove Costly for Ukraine
2022-06-28.abstract: The Ukrainian Army has tried to modernize, but still falls short in coordination among units, so they often fight independently of one another — with sometimes deadly results.

2022-06-29.title: Zelensky urges the U.N. to label Russia a terrorist state over a missile strike on a shopping mall.
2022-06-29.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine addressed the United Nations Security Council on Tuesday, calling for the organization to brand Russia a terrorist state and dispatch investigators to inspect a missile attack on a shopping mall a day earlier in the city of Kremenchuk.

2022-06-29.title: After Rapes by Russian Soldiers, a Painful Quest for Justice
2022-06-29.abstract: Women who were attacked in a village near Kyiv yearn for justice. “I want them to be punished,” said one victim. But Ukrainian officials face daunting challenges in prosecuting such crimes.

2022-06-29.title: NATO formally invites Finland and Sweden to join the alliance.
2022-06-29.abstract: The historic deal underscores how the war in Ukraine has backfired for President Vladimir V. Putin.

2022-06-29.title: ‘Very Fragile’: Shanghai Wrestles With Psychological Scars of Lockdown
2022-06-29.abstract: The lockdown fueled anxiety, fear and depression among the city’s residents. Experts have warned that the mental health impact of the confinement will be long-lasting.

2022-06-29.title: Israel’s Spies Have Hit Iran Hard. In Tehran, Some Big Names Paid the Price.
2022-06-29.abstract: After a series of damaging failures, a senior Iranian intelligence official lost his job and a Revolutionary Guards general was said to have been arrested.

2022-06-30.title: Patient and Confident, Putin Shifts Out of Wartime Crisis Mode
2022-06-30.abstract: Cloistered and spouting grievances at the start of the war on Ukraine, the Russian leader now appears publicly, projecting the aura of a calm, paternalistic leader shielding his people from the dangers of the world.

2022-06-30.title: Israel’s Parliament Dissolves, Paving Way for 5th Election in 4 Years
2022-06-30.abstract: The decision brought down the coalition government and installed Yair Lapid, a centrist, as interim prime minister. Exhausted and exasperated Israelis will be asked to vote again in November.

2022-06-30.title: Vitaliy Kim, Master Motivator and Symbol of Ukraine’s Resistance
2022-06-30.abstract: Applying the lessons of taekwondo, the regional military leader has rallied the embattled southern city of Mykolaiv.

2022-06-30.title: China’s Leader Hails a Hong Kong ‘Reborn From Ashes’ Amid Crackdown
2022-06-30.abstract: Before Xi Jinping’s tightly controlled appearance, Hong Kong sent officials, diplomats and others to hotels for days of isolation and Covid tests.
